data.title: New Century Healthcare Refreshes Board With Independent Healthcare Investor
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. ( (HK:1518) ) just unveiled an announcement.

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. announced changes to its board, with non-executive director Mr. Wang Siye resigning effective June 30, 2026, citing work adjustments. The board noted that Mr. Wang has no disagreements with directors and expressed gratitude for his contributions, indicating a smooth and orderly transition in governance.

The company appointed Mr. Qiu Xiang as a new non-executive director effective July 2, 2026, bringing extensive healthcare investment experience and multiple directorships in medical and biotech firms. Mr. Qiu will serve an initial three-year term without remuneration, reinforcing the board’s focus on sector expertise and potentially strengthening New Century Healthcare’s strategic positioning and investor confidence.

The board stated that Mr. Qiu has no prior roles within the group, no relationships with existing management or major shareholders, and no shareholding in the company, underscoring his independence. This appointment aligns with listing rule requirements and highlights the firm’s attention to corporate governance, transparency, and oversight as it continues to operate in the competitive Chinese healthcare market.

More about New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd.

New Century Healthcare Holding Co. Ltd. operates in the healthcare sector, providing medical services in China. The company is listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and is overseen by a board that includes executive and non-executive directors, reflecting its focus on governance and strategic oversight in a regulated market.

The group’s leadership structure incorporates non-executive directors from investment and healthcare-related backgrounds. This board composition underscores its emphasis on industry expertise and capital markets experience as it navigates the evolving Chinese healthcare landscape and regulatory environment.

Average Trading Volume: 143,291

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$195.2M

data.title: LongBio Pharma Ends Global Offering Stabilization With Full Over-allotment Exercise
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An announcement from LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1779) ) is now available.

LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company incorporated in the PRC, has structured its capital raising through a Global Offering of H shares to investors outside the United States under Regulation S. The company’s listing involves both a Hong Kong Public Offering and an International Offering, reflecting its ambition to broaden its investor base and strengthen financial resources for its operations.

The company announced that the stabilization period for its Global Offering ended on July 2, 2026, the 30th day after the last date for lodging applications under the Hong Kong Public Offering. This marks the formal close of post-offering price support activities associated with its recent share sale.

During the stabilization period, Sinolink Securities (Hong Kong) Company Limited, acting as Stabilizing Manager, over-allocated 2,128,950 H shares in the International Offering, accounting for about 15% of the total offer shares initially available. This over-allocation mechanism is commonly used to support orderly trading and manage demand in the immediate aftermarket.

The Sole Sponsor-Overall Coordinator fully exercised the over-allotment option on June 30, 2026, in respect of the same 2,128,950 H shares at an offer price of HK$96.06 per share, excluding applicable fees and levies. This exercise allowed the company and its underwriters to facilitate delivery of H shares to placees who had agreed to delayed settlement under the Global Offering.

No purchases or sales of H shares were conducted in the open market by the Stabilizing Manager for the purpose of price stabilization during the stabilization period. The completion of stabilization without market trades suggests that aftermarket demand and trading in LongBio Pharma’s H shares may have been sufficiently robust to obviate the need for active price support.

The conclusion of the stabilization period and full exercise of the over-allotment option indicate that the Global Offering was fully subscribed and operationally successful. For stakeholders, including shareholders and prospective investors, the outcome provides clarity on the final size of the offering and underscores the company’s ability to execute a complex cross-border equity issuance within Hong Kong’s regulatory framework.

More about LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. Class H

LongBio Pharma (Suzhou) Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China with limited liability and listed in Hong Kong as Class H shares under stock code 1779. The company operates in the pharmaceutical sector, focusing on the development and commercialization of biopharmaceutical products, and accesses international capital markets through global offerings of its H shares.

Average Trading Volume: 489,217

Current Market Cap: HK$11.01B

Learn more about 1779 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: HKBN Revises China Mobile Linked Agreements and Caps After Year-End Change
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

HKBN ( (HK:1310) ) has shared an update.

HKBN has entered into 2026 supplemental agreements with CMHK and CMI to amend the scope of services under existing master agreements and renew their term, while revising annual transaction caps following a change in its financial year-end from 31 August to 31 December. These agreements cover a broad suite of services, including fixed-line, mobile backhaul, data centre and international telecommunications, to be provided between HKBN and the China Mobile Group.

Because CMHK and CMI are connected persons under Hong Kong Listing Rules, the amended arrangements and revised caps are treated as continuing connected transactions, triggering specific compliance requirements under Chapter 14A. Most services fall either into fully exempt or partially exempt categories, meaning they are subject to reporting, annual review and announcement obligations but do not require independent shareholder approval, and HKBN has appointed an independent financial adviser to confirm that multi-year terms exceeding three years are consistent with normal business practice.

More about HKBN

HKBN Ltd. is a Hong Kong-based telecommunications and technology services provider, offering fixed-line, mobile, data centre, international telecommunications and technology solutions. The company operates within the Hong Kong and Greater China connectivity markets, and is majority controlled by China Mobile through its subsidiary CMHK, which holds 74.84% of HKBN’s issued share capital.

HKBN’s services include fixed telecommunications network services, mobile backhaul, data centre hosting, international telecoms and consultancy-driven technology solutions for enterprise and carrier customers. Its close operational ties with the China Mobile Group position HKBN as a key partner in regional network infrastructure and cross-border connectivity services.

YTD Price Performance: -2.53%

data.title: Offerors Secure Majority Control in CNT Group as Takeover Closes
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CNT Group Limited ( (HK:0701) ) has issued an announcement.

CNT Group Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 701, has been the subject of a formal takeover offer by Prime Surplus Limited and SHK Hong Kong Industries Limited. The Offerors and their concert parties entered the offer period with a significant minority stake, positioning themselves to move into effective control through incremental share acquisitions.

The companies announced the close of the CNT offer on 2 July 2026, confirming it was not revised or extended and had become unconditional after acceptance levels and existing holdings lifted their stake above 50%. Following valid acceptances for 303,301,869 CNT shares during the offer period, the Offerors and concert parties will hold about 50.31% of CNT’s issued share capital, marking a transition to majority ownership and altering the public float and governance dynamics for remaining shareholders.

More about CNT Group Limited

CNT Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 701. The company is the target of a takeover offer by Prime Surplus Limited and SHK Hong Kong Industries Limited, which have been steadily increasing their stake through a general offer to existing shareholders.

Prior to the offer period beginning on 21 April 2026, the Offerors and their concert parties already controlled 34.38% of CNT’s issued shares. Their campaign has now pushed their combined holding beyond the 50% threshold, giving them majority control and reshaping the ownership structure of the listed group.

One Media Group Limited ( (HK:0426) ) has provided an update.

One Media Group Limited plans to overhaul its constitutional documents, proposing amendments to its existing memorandum and articles of association and adopting a new set to replace them in full. The changes aim to enable shareholders to attend and vote at general meetings virtually, align the company’s governing documents with Hong Kong’s latest paperless listing and regulatory requirements, and support the transition to an uncertificated securities market, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming AGM.

A circular detailing the proposed amendments and the adoption of the new memorandum and articles, together with the notice of the annual general meeting, will be sent to shareholders in due course. The move underscores the company’s effort to modernise its governance framework and meeting procedures, potentially improving shareholder engagement and operational efficiency in line with evolving market infrastructure and listing rules.

More about One Media Group Limited

One Media Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (Stock Code: 426), operates in the media sector. data.title: China Shenhua Brings New Hebei Coal Units into Commercial Operation
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

China Shenhua Energy Co ( (HK:1088) ) has shared an announcement.

China Shenhua Energy has announced that the No. 5 power generation units at the Phase III expansion projects of both Dingzhou and Cangdong power plants in Hebei Province have successfully completed their 168-hour trial runs and entered commercial operation. Each project comprises two 660 MW ultra-supercritical coal-fired units operated by 51%-owned subsidiaries, reflecting the company’s ongoing push to expand high-efficiency thermal capacity.

Design parameters for the new units indicate relatively low coal consumption for power supply, underscoring a focus on operational efficiency within traditional coal generation. With work on the respective No. 6 units progressing and commercial operations expected soon, full commissioning of both projects is set to ease peak-load and heat-supply pressure on the local grid and strengthen energy security across the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei region, reinforcing China Shenhua’s strategic role in regional power reliability.

Both Dingzhou Phase III and Cangdong Phase III are designated as key energy security projects under Hebei’s 14th Five-Year Plan, highlighting their policy significance. Their successful ramp-up marks a milestone in the province’s effort to secure stable electricity and heating, while for China Shenhua the added capacity enhances its position in China’s coal-based power sector and has implications for regulators, local governments and grid operators reliant on steady baseload supply.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1088) stock is a Hold
with a HK$36.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Shenhua Energy Co stock,
see the HK:1088 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Shenhua Energy Co

China Shenhua Energy Company Limited is one of China’s largest integrated energy producers, focusing on coal-fired power generation alongside coal mining and related operations. Through majority-owned subsidiaries, the company operates key power plants that support regional grid stability and energy security, particularly in northern China’s Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei area.

The group’s expansion projects often involve high-efficiency, ultra-supercritical coal-fired units designed to reduce coal consumption per kilowatt-hour while bolstering capacity. These investments align with provincial and national planning priorities, positioning China Shenhua as a central player in ensuring reliable power supply during peak demand and heating seasons.

Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed healthcare operator based in Zhejiang, China, derives its funding from a mix of domestic shares and H shares, enabling participation by both mainland and overseas investors. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent directors in line with cross-border listing requirements.

The company announced that China’s securities regulator has accepted and completed its filing application for the full circulation, conversion and listing of its shares, paving the way for more domestic shares to be converted into H shares. Following the planned conversion, the proportion of domestic shares will fall from about 72.91% to 60.08%, while H shares will rise to 39.92%, though the implementation plan and timetable remain unfinalised and subject to further regulatory procedures, prompting a cautionary note to shareholders and potential investors.

More about Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co

The company’s share capital is split between domestic shares and H shares, giving it access to both mainland and international investors through the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Its governance structure includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting standard corporate oversight for a cross-border listed healthcare group. The company’s capital structure and listing status are subject to regulation by both Hong Kong and mainland Chinese securities authorities, including the CSRC.

Huajin International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2738) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Huajin International Holdings has disclosed that equity interests in certain lower-tier mainland China subsidiaries have been judicially frozen in 52 instances, following asset preservation applications by external third-party claimants. The freezes stem from outstanding payment obligations owed by these PRC units to various creditors.

The company stressed that the measures apply only to the equity of its PRC subsidiaries and do not affect its listed shares in Hong Kong, which remain unfrozen according to official records. Management is continuing cross-checks across government and judicial portals to verify all freezing orders and identify any additional records, aiming to keep shareholders and potential investors informed of the evolving situation.

More about Huajin International Holdings Ltd.

Huajin International Holdings Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a network of subsidiaries in mainland China. The group’s PRC entities are involved in commercial activities that generate payment obligations to various external creditors, exposing them to civil and commercial disputes in the Chinese courts.

data.title: United Laboratories Wins China Approval for New Tadalafil Men’s Health Drug
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The United Laboratories International Holdings ( (HK:3933) ) has shared an announcement.

The United Laboratories International Holdings has received Chinese regulatory approval to market its Tadalafil Tablets in 5mg, 10mg and 20mg strengths, developed by its wholly owned Zhuhai United Laboratories Zhongshan Branch. The product is a selective PDE5 inhibitor for erectile dysfunction, including cases associated with benign prostatic hyperplasia, aligning the company more deeply with the fast-growing men’s health segment in China.

Tadalafil’s prolonged duration of action of up to 36 hours and efficacy unaffected by food intake are expected to provide patients with greater flexibility and improve the drug’s clinical appeal relative to peers. The company says the new approval further enriches its product portfolio and should bolster its competitiveness and brand influence in men’s health, while ongoing investment in R&D aims to strengthen its innovation capacity and long-term value creation for shareholders.

The move underscores the group’s strategy of expanding into high-demand therapeutic areas where differentiated formulations can support both clinical outcomes and market positioning. Stakeholders may see the approval as a signal of continued pipeline productivity and regulatory execution, potentially improving the firm’s standing in China’s crowded pharmaceutical landscape.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3933) stock is a Buy
with a HK$20.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The United Laboratories International Holdings stock,
see the HK:3933 Stock Forecast page.

More about The United Laboratories International Holdings

The United Laboratories International Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed pharmaceutical group incorporated in the Cayman Islands, focusing on the research, development and manufacturing of prescription drugs. Through subsidiaries such as Zhuhai United Laboratories, the company targets key therapeutic areas including men’s health, aiming to broaden its diversified product portfolio and strengthen competitiveness in the Chinese pharmaceutical market.

The group leverages its wholly owned production base in Zhuhai and Zhongshan to develop branded formulations, seeking to enhance its presence in specialized segments. By continually investing in innovation and expanding its range of approved medicines, the company positions itself to capture growing demand in China’s healthcare sector and deliver long-term value to shareholders.

Its strategy emphasizes combining clinical advantages with market differentiation, as seen in products designed for enhanced patient convenience and efficacy. This approach supports the company’s bid to reinforce its brand influence and secure a larger share of targeted therapeutic markets.

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co ( (HK:1349) ) has shared an update.

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co has subscribed to a principal-guaranteed structured deposit product from Bank of China, investing RMB150 million of temporarily idle proceeds raised from its public A-share issuance. The 90-day deposit, assessed by the bank as low-risk, offers a floating annualized return between 0.3500% and 1.6500%, linked to movements in the USD/CAD central parity rate.

China Datang Corp. Renewable Power Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1798) ) has shared an announcement.

China Datang Corporation Renewable Power Co. Ltd. has announced the upcoming payment of interest on its third tranche of 2025 mid-term notes, a RMB1 billion bond issue in China’s interbank market. The notes carry a 2.01% coupon, with interest for the period from 9 July 2025 to 8 July 2026 scheduled to be paid on 9 July 2026, underscoring the company’s adherence to its debt obligations and providing clarity to bondholders on cash flow timing.

The interest payment timetable, aligned with mainland Chinese working days, highlights the issuer’s effort to maintain transparent communication with investors across both domestic and Hong Kong markets. This routine servicing of onshore debt supports confidence in the company’s financial discipline and may reinforce its standing in the renewable power bond market as it continues to finance growth through fixed-income instruments.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1798) stock is a Hold
with a HK$2.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Datang Corp. Renewable Power Co. Ltd. Class H stock,
see the HK:1798 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Datang Corp. Renewable Power Co. Ltd. Class H

China Datang Corp. Renewable Power Co. Ltd. Class H is a Hong Kong-listed subsidiary in China’s renewable energy sector, focusing on power generation from clean sources. The company taps onshore Chinese debt markets to fund operations, using mid-term notes to support its capital structure and ongoing development of renewable power assets.

data.title: Fudan Zhangjiang Boosts Yield on Idle Cash With RMB210 Million SPD Bank Deposit
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co ( (HK:1349) ).

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co has subscribed to a principal-guaranteed structured deposit product with SPD Bank, investing RMB210 million of idle self-owned funds generated from daily operations. The 88-day product is assessed as low-risk, offers a floating annualized return between 1.00% and 1.95%, and links its yield to EUR/USD movements, providing the company with modest income on surplus cash.

Under Hong Kong Listing Rules, the size of this investment causes one or more applicable percentage ratios to exceed 5% but remain below 25%, classifying the transaction as a disclosable transaction requiring reporting and public announcement without shareholder approval. The move underscores the company’s conservative treasury management, balancing capital preservation with enhanced returns, and signals disciplined liquidity deployment for stakeholders monitoring its financial strategy.

More about Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co

Shanghai Fudan Zhangjiang Bio Pharmaceutical Co operates in the biopharmaceutical industry, focusing on the research, development and commercialization of pharmaceutical products. The company uses financial instruments to manage its liquidity, allocating idle operational funds into low-risk, principal-guaranteed structured deposit products to optimize returns while preserving capital.

Its market focus includes maintaining a stable financial position to support ongoing operations and investments in drug development. By engaging in short-term, low-risk banking products, the company seeks incremental yield on surplus cash without compromising liquidity or risk management standards.

Average Trading Volume: 2,680,614

Current Market Cap: HK$772.6M

data.title: Integrated Waste Solutions Announces CEO Transition and Leadership Re-Designation
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:0923) ).

Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited has announced a leadership transition effective 1 September 2026, with long-serving executive director and chief executive officer Mr. Lam King Sang retiring from all his roles in the group to devote more time to personal commitments. The board emphasized there is no disagreement with Mr. Lam and expressed appreciation for his decade of service, signaling a planned and orderly handover rather than a dispute-driven change.

On the same effective date, the company will elevate its chief financial officer and executive director, Mr. Tam Sui Kin, Chris, to the role of chief executive officer, leveraging his more than 35 years of experience in auditing, accounting, project financing and financial management. The move places a seasoned finance professional at the helm, which may influence the group’s strategic focus on financial discipline and capital management as it navigates future growth and industry challenges.

More about Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited

Integrated Waste Solutions Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed environmental services company focused on integrated waste management solutions through its group of subsidiaries. The company operates in the environmental and recycling sector, providing waste treatment and related services to support sustainable resource management in Hong Kong and the region.

Average Trading Volume: 1,805,959

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$91.64M

data.title: AINNOVATION Technology Steps Up Share Buy-Backs to Signal Confidence
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

AInnovation Technology Group ( (HK:2121) ) has shared an update.

AINNOVATION Technology Group has updated investors on its previously announced share repurchase programme, authorized under a general mandate of up to HK$100 million approved at its May 2026 annual general meeting. The technology company is using on-market buy-backs as a capital management tool, signaling its intent to actively manage its share base in line with Hong Kong’s regulatory framework.

In June 2026, the company repurchased a total of 961,600 shares on the open market for a consideration of about HK$4.04 million, and indicated that further repurchases may be conducted subject to market conditions and board discretion. The board said the buy-back activity reflects confidence in the firm’s business outlook and financial strength, and is expected to enhance shareholder value, while cautioning investors that the timing, volume and pricing of future repurchases are not assured.

More about AInnovation Technology Group

AINNOVATION Technology Group Co., Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed, PRC-incorporated technology company whose shares trade on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 2121. The company operates in the broader innovation and technology sector, with its shareholder base including institutional and retail investors focused on Chinese tech growth and capital market developments.

The company’s governance structure features an executive director and CEO, supported by non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate board composition. Its listing status subjects it to Hong Kong’s securities, takeover, and share buy-back regulations, which shape its capital management strategies and investor communications.
